summary: Kimi Antonelli's maiden pole position sets up a Mercedes power play at Spa, with the rookie's blistering pace leaving Max Verstappen second and teammate George Russell fourth on the grid. Lando Norris charges upward from a 10-place penalty while Ferrari seeks momentum following Lewis Hamilton's confidence-building victory in Spain, creating a fractured field where track position becomes paramount on Spa's high-speed straights. A strong result here could reshape the championship narrative for Mercedes and Ferrari ahead of the season's second half, particularly if Antonelli converts pole into a maiden Grand Prix win.
